title: Sclerolaena symoniana
taxonomy: Sclerolaena symoniana is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. It belongs to the genus Sclerolaena, which consists of various species of shrubs and herbs. The classification of Sclerolaena symoniana places it within the order Caryophyllales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The family Amaranthaceae is a family of flowering plants that includes a wide range of species, many of which are commonly known as pigweeds or amaranths. Further classification of Sclerolaena symoniana places it within the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants and green algae.

intro: Sclerolaena symoniana, also known as Symon's Sclerolaena, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage.
